title: arenaria ciliata subsp. tatrensis
intro: Arenaria ciliata subsp. tatrensis, also known as Tatrensky Winter-cress or Tatrensky Arenaria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family, Caryophyllaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Arenaria ciliata subsp. tatrensis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce oxidative stress, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create teas, tinctures, or infusions that can be used to support overall health and well-being.
